questions.q: The time is 9:20 A.M. What is the correct way of transmitting this time if there is no possibility of confusion about the hour?
options: Twenty
Two zero this hour
Two zero
Nine twenty A.M.
questions.explanation: Se non vi e rischio di confusione sull'ora, si puo trasmettere solo la cifra dei minuti: le 9:20 diventano 'two zero'.

questions.q: What action is required by the pilot of an aircraft station if he/she is unable to establish radio contact with an aeronautical station ?
options: Try to establish communication with other aircraft or aeronautical stations
Divert to the alternate airport
Squawk mode A code 7500
Land at the nearest aerodrome appropriate to the route of flight
questions.explanation: Se non si riesce a stabilire il contatto con una stazione aeronautica, il pilota deve tentare di comunicare con altri aeromobili o altre stazioni.

questions.q: What action should be taken by the aircraft station first receiving a distress message?
options: Request position of the station in distress
Ask the station to change to frequency 121.5 MHZ.
Immediately acknowledge the distress message
Maintain radio silence until called
questions.explanation: La prima stazione che riceve un messaggio di soccorso deve confermarne immediatamente la ricezione.

questions.q: An aircraft is squawking 7600. This indicates:
options: It is diverting to the alternate aerodrome
It is requesting immediate level change
It is about to make a forced landing
It is unable to establish communication due to radio equipment failure
questions.explanation: Il codice 7600 sul transponder indica un'avaria alle comunicazioni radio.

questions.q: What does the term `aeronautical station` mean ?
options: Any station established to exchange radiotelephony communications
Station in the aeronautical telecommunication service located on land or on board of an aircraft to exchange radiotelephony communications
Station in the aeronautical mobile service located on land or, in certain instances, on board of a ship or on a platform at sea
An airborne station forming part of the aeronautical fixed telecommunication network (AFTN)
questions.explanation: Una 'aeronautical station' e una stazione del servizio mobile aeronautico situata a terra, o in certi casi a bordo di una nave o piattaforma in mare, usata per le comunicazioni radiotelefoniche.

questions.q: Which is the frequency separation between consecutive frequencies in the VHF band:
options: 50 KHz
75 KHz
25 KHz
250 KHz
questions.explanation: La spaziatura tra canali consecutivi in banda VHF aeronautica e di 25 kHz.

questions.q: Urgency is defined as:
options: A condition concerning the safety of a person on board or within sight and requiring immediate assistance
A condition concerning the attitude of an aircraft when intercepting the localizer during an ILS approach
A condition concerning the safety of an aircraft other vehicles or of a person on board, but which does not require immediate assistance
A condition of being threatened by serious and/or imminent danger and of requiring immediate assistance
questions.explanation: L''urgency' riguarda la sicurezza di un aeromobile, di un altro veicolo o di una persona a bordo, ma senza necessita di assistenza immediata (a differenza del 'distress').

questions.q: If all attempts to establish radio contact with a ground station fail, the pilot of an aeroplane shall transmit messages preceded by the phrase:
options: `Transmitting blind`
`Read you one, read you one`
`How do you read?`
PAN PAN, PAN PAN, PAN PAN
questions.explanation: Se tutti i tentativi di contatto con una stazione a terra falliscono, i messaggi vanno preceduti dalla frase 'transmitting blind'.

questions.q: Which of these statements best describes the meaning of the phrase `Standby` ?
options: Continue on present heading and listen out
Select STANDBY on the SSR transponder
Wait and I will call you
Permission granted for action proposed
questions.explanation: 'Standby' significa attendere: la stazione chiamante sara richiamata appena possibile.

questions.q: RADAR informs aircraft X-BC: `X-BC identified`. What does this mean:
options: Radar identification has been achieved
X-BC is not visible on the radar screen
X-BC should perform an identification turn
X-BC should operate the IDENT-button
questions.explanation: 'X-BC identified' significa che l'identificazione radar dell'aeromobile e stata raggiunta (correlazione tra blip e traffico noto).

questions.q: What does the term `blind transmission` mean?
options: A transmission of information relating to air navigation that is not addressed to a specific station or stations
A transmission from one station to another station in circumstances where two-way communication cannot be established but it is believed that the called station is able to receive the transmission
A transmission of messages relating to en-route weather information which may affect the safety of aircraft operations that is not addressed to a specific station or stations
A transmission where no reply is required from the receiving station
questions.explanation: Una 'blind transmission' e una trasmissione effettuata quando non si puo stabilire un contatto bidirezionale, ma si ritiene che la stazione chiamata sia comunque in grado di riceverla.

questions.q: A message concerning aircraft parts and material urgently required is:
options: A flight regularity message
An urgency message
A flight safety message.
A flight security message.
questions.explanation: Un messaggio su parti di ricambio urgentemente necessarie per un aeromobile e classificato come messaggio di urgenza (urgency message).

questions.q: When transmitting time, which time system shall be used?
options: Local time (LT), 24-hour clock
Local time (LT) A.M. and P.M.
Co-ordinated universal time (UTC)
No specific system, as only the minutes are normally required
questions.explanation: Nella trasmissione degli orari si utilizza sempre il tempo coordinato universale (UTC).

questions.q: Which is the maximum distance at which you might expect solid VHF contact over flat terrain at flight level 50:
options: About 85 NM
About 8 NM
About 15 NM
About 150 NM
questions.explanation: Su terreno pianeggiante, a FL50 il contatto VHF solido e possibile fino a circa 85 NM, per effetto della curvatura terrestre sulla propagazione ottica del segnale.

questions.q: Which of the following frequencies is an international emergency frequency:
options: 6500 KHz
questions.explanation: 121.500 MHz e la frequenza internazionale di emergenza per le comunicazioni aeronautiche.

questions.q: What is `VOLMET`?
options: Meteorological information for aircraft in flight.
Meteorological station
Volume control meter.
A device used for measuring cloud base.
questions.explanation: Il VOLMET fornisce informazioni meteorologiche destinate agli aeromobili in volo.

questions.q: An aircraft station fails to establish radio contact with an aeronautical station on the designated frequency. What action is required by the pilot:
options: Continue the flight to the destination airport without any communication
Attempt to establish contact with the station on an alternative frequency
Return to the airport of departure
Land at the nearest airport without an ATC unit
questions.explanation: Se il contatto sulla frequenza designata fallisce, occorre tentare di stabilirlo su una frequenza alternativa.

questions.q: What does the instruction `Vacate left` mean ?
options: Turn left to leave the runway
Give way to aircraft from the left
Clear the runway immediately
Hold position on the left side of the runway
questions.explanation: 'Vacate left' istruisce l'aeromobile a lasciare la pista girando a sinistra.

questions.q: Which abbreviation is used for `Co-ordinated universal time`?
options: CUT
GMT
UTC
COUT
questions.explanation: 'UTC' e l'abbreviazione standard per il tempo coordinato universale (Co-ordinated Universal Time).

questions.q: Which phrase shall be used if you want to say: `I understand your message and will comply with it`:
options: Roger
Will comply with your instruction
OK, will do it
Wilco
questions.explanation: 'Wilco' significa che il messaggio e stato compreso e che ci si attera alle istruzioni ricevute.

questions.q: Which of the messages listed below shall not be handled by the aeronautical mobile service?
options: Meteorological messages.
Flight safety messages.
Radio teletype messages
Urgency messages
questions.explanation: I messaggi radiotelex (radio teletype) non rientrano tra quelli gestiti dal servizio mobile aeronautico vocale.

questions.q: What does the instruction `Go around` mean ?
options: Overtake the aircraft ahead
Proceed with your message
Make a 360 degree turn
Carry out a missed approach
questions.explanation: 'Go around' indica l'esecuzione di un mancato avvicinamento.

questions.q: Which abbreviation is used for the term `control zone`?
options: CZ
CTR
CTZ
CTA
questions.explanation: 'CTR' e l'abbreviazione standard per 'control zone' (zona di controllo).

questions.q: During the transmission of numbers containing a decimal point:
options: The term DECIMAL can be omitted with friendly ATS units only.
The term DECIMAL can be omitted if no chance of misunderstanding exists.
The term DECIMAL must be spoken only if followed by three digits.
The term DECIMAL must always be transmitted.
questions.explanation: Il termine 'decimal' va sempre pronunciato quando si trasmette un numero con la virgola/punto decimale, per evitare ambiguita (es. frequenze).

questions.q: What is the radiotelephony call sign for the aeronautical station providing surface movement control of aircraft on the manoeuvring area:
options: APPROACH
TOWER
CONTROL
GROUND
questions.explanation: Il nominativo radiotelefonico per il controllo dei movimenti superficie sull'area di manovra e 'GROUND'.

questions.q: Under which of the following circumstances shall an aircraft
options: When following a SID
When flying within controlled airspace
When passing the transition level
In distress
questions.explanation: Tale condotta si applica in situazioni di pericolo (distress), quando e necessaria assistenza immediata.

questions.q: The priority of the instruction `taxi to runway 05` is:
options: The same as `line-up runway 07 and wait`.
Greater than `transmit for QDM`.
Greater than `caution, construction work left of taxiway`.
Ess than `cleared to land`.
questions.explanation: L'istruzione 'taxi to runway 05' ha la stessa priorita di 'line-up runway 07 and wait', trattandosi entrambe di istruzioni di movimento a terra dello stesso livello operativo.

questions.q: My message will be more effective and understandable if I:
options: Maintain the speaking volume at a constant level
Use the words twice method
Stress the end of message
Stress every beginning of message
questions.explanation: Un messaggio risulta piu efficace mantenendo un volume di voce costante durante tutta la trasmissione, evitando di enfatizzare solo inizio o fine frase.

questions.q: Any frequency at pilot's discretion
options: MAYDAY, MAYDAY, MAYDAY
DETRESFA, DETRESFA, DETRESFA
PAN PAN, PAN PAN, PAN PAN
URGENCY, URGENCY, URGENCY
questions.explanation: Il segnale di soccorso MAYDAY, ripetuto tre volte, apre sempre un messaggio di pericolo e ha priorita assoluta su qualsiasi altra comunicazione.

questions.q: Aircraft X-BC has been instructed to contact Stephenville TOWER on frequency 118.7. What is the correct response to indicate that it will follow this instruction ?
options: Will change to TOWER X-BC
Changing over X-BC
Stephenville TOWER X-BC
questions.explanation: La risposta corretta ripete la frequenza assegnata seguita dal proprio nominativo (118.7 X-BC), confermando il cambio di frequenza.

questions.q: Which elements of instructions or information shall always be read back ?
options: Runway-in-use, visibility, surface wind, heading instructions, altimeter settings
Runway-in-use, altimeter settings, SSR codes, level instructions, heading and speed instructions
Surface wind, visibility, ground temperature, runway-in-use, altimeter settings, heading and speedinstructions
Time check, runway-in-use, altimeter settings, level instructions, SSR codes
questions.explanation: Gli elementi sempre da ripetere (read-back) sono: pista in uso, impostazioni altimetriche, codici SSR, istruzioni di livello e istruzioni di prua/velocita.

questions.q: From your cruising altitude at FL 240, you want to descend to flight level 100. Your transmission to the radar controller is:
options: Request flight level one-hundred
Request descent to flight level one-zero-zero
Request to descend one-hundred
We would like to start descent to flight level one-zero-zero
questions.explanation: La richiesta di discesa si esprime con 'request descent to flight level...' seguito dal livello desiderato cifra per cifra.

questions.q: How shall a pilot inform a radar control unit that the aircraft is not equipped with transponder:
options: No SSR
Transponder not available
Negative squawk
Negative transponder
questions.explanation: 'Negative transponder' comunica al controllore radar che l'aeromobile non e equipaggiato con apparato transponder.

questions.q: How shall a pilot ask for a QFE ?
options: Request Queen Fox Easy
Request Quebec Fox Easy
Request Quebec Foxtrot Echo
Request Quebec Fox Echo
questions.explanation: Nell'alfabeto fonetico ICAO, F si dice 'Foxtrot': QFE si compita 'Quebec Foxtrot Echo'.

questions.q: What are the propagation characteristics of VHF:
options: The waves are reflected at the ionosphere at the height of about 100 km and reach the earth surface in the form of skywaves
The waves travel along the surface of the earth and penetrate into valleys in a way that topographical obstacles have no influence
Similar to short waves with practically no atmospheric disturbance
Practically straight-line similar to light waves
questions.explanation: Le onde VHF si propagano in linea pressoche retta, similmente alla luce, e non si riflettono sulla ionosfera come le onde corte.

questions.q: What is the transponder code for radio communication failure:
questions.explanation: Il codice transponder 7600 indica un'avaria alle comunicazioni radio.

questions.q: Which phraseology is to be used to ask the control tower for permission to taxi on a runway in the direction opposite to that in use ?
options: `Clearance to backtrack`.
To enter back runway`.
`Backtrack clearance`.
`Request backtrack on runway`.
questions.explanation: 'Request backtrack on runway' e la fraseologia standard per chiedere alla torre l'autorizzazione a rullare sulla pista in direzione opposta a quella in uso.

questions.q: When transmitting a message preceded by the phrase `Transmitting blind due to receiver failure` during an en-route flight, the aircraft station shall also:
options: Join base leg when approaching the airfield for landing
Land at the nearest airfield/airporta
Advise the time of its next intended transmission
Return to the airport of departure
questions.explanation: Durante una blind transmission in rotta per avaria al ricevente, l'aeromobile deve anche comunicare l'orario della successiva trasmissione prevista.

questions.q: What is the Q-code for `magnetic heading to the station (no wind)?
options: QDR
QDM
QNE
QTE
questions.explanation: QDM e il Q-code per la prua magnetica verso la stazione (senza vento).

questions.q: How should aircraft XY-ABC call Stephenville TOWER on initial call ?
options: Stephenville TOWER X-BC
Stephenville XY-ABC
TOWER XY-ABC
Stephenville TOWER XY-ABC
questions.explanation: Alla prima chiamata, sia la stazione a terra che l'aeromobile si identificano con il nominativo completo (Stephenville TOWER, XY-ABC), senza abbreviazioni.

questions.q: An aircraft is instructed to hold short of the runway-in-use. What is the correct phraseology to indicate it will follow this instruction ?
options: Roger
Will stop before
Holding short
Wilco
questions.explanation: 'Holding short' e la fraseologia corretta per confermare l'attesa prima dell'ingresso in pista.

questions.q: The message addressed to an Area Control Centre `request radar vectors to circumnavigate adverse weather` is:
options: A meteorological message
An urgency message
A flight safety message
A message relating to direction finding.
questions.explanation: Una richiesta di vettori radar per evitare condizioni meteo avverse e classificata come messaggio di sicurezza del volo.

questions.q: The order of priority of the following messages in the aeronautical mobile service is:
options: Direction finding message, distress message, urgency message
Distress message, flight safety message, urgency message
Distress message, urgency message, direction finding message.
Meteorological message, direction finding message, flight regularity message
questions.explanation: L'ordine di priorita dei messaggi nel servizio mobile aeronautico e: soccorso (distress), urgenza (urgency), radiogoniometria (direction finding).

questions.q: Which phrase shall be used to confirm that a message has been repeated correctly:
options: Correct
That is right
Affirm
That is affirmative
questions.explanation: 'Correct' e la fraseologia standard per confermare che un messaggio e stato ripetuto esattamente.

questions.q: .How is the visibility in an aviation routine weather report (METAR) expressed in plain language:
options: Up to 5000 m in metres, above in kilometres
Up to 1500 m in metres, above in kilometres
In feet and nautical miles
In nautical miles only
questions.explanation: Nel METAR in linguaggio semplice, la visibilita si esprime in metri fino a 5000 m, oltre tale soglia in chilometri.

questions.q: What is meant by good microphone technique ?
options: Speak very loudly into the microphone.
Keep the microphone far away since it improves the readability
Make large use of hesitation sounds as `er`
Use a normal conversation tone, speak clearly and distinctly
questions.explanation: Una buona tecnica microfonica prevede un tono di voce normale, con pronuncia chiara e distinta, senza gridare ne allontanare troppo il microfono.

questions.q: Which phrase shall be used if you want to say: `Pass me the following information...`:
options: Request
Say again
Report
Check
questions.explanation: 'Report' e la fraseologia standard per chiedere di trasmettere una determinata informazione.

questions.q: The priority of the pilot's message `request QDM` is:
options: Less than `request climb to flight level...`
Greater than `turn left heading...`
Less than `descend to flight level...`
Same as `latest QNH 1018`.
questions.explanation: Una richiesta di QDM ha priorita maggiore di un'istruzione di virata ('turn left heading...'), trattandosi di un'informazione di sicurezza della navigazione.

questions.q: Distress is defined as:
options: A condition of being threatened by serious and/or imminent danger and requiring immediate assistance
A condition concerning the safety of an aircraft or of a person on board, but which does not require immediateassistance
A condition concerning the attitude of an aircraft when intercepting the localizer during an ILS approach
A condition concerning the safety of a person on board or within sight and requiring immediate assistance
questions.explanation: Il 'distress' e una condizione di pericolo grave e/o imminente che richiede assistenza immediata.

questions.q: How can aviation routine weather reports (METAR) of specific airports be obtained by aircraft in flight:
options: VOLMET
ATIS
AFIS
SIGMET
questions.explanation: I METAR di aeroporti specifici sono ottenibili in volo tramite il servizio VOLMET.

questions.q: Aircraft XY-ABC is making a test transmission with Stephenville TOWER on frequency 118.7. What is the correct phrasing for this transmission:
options: Stephenville TOWER XY-ABC pre-flight check
Stephenville TOWER XY-ABC signal check
Stephenville TOWER XY-ABC radio check 118.7
Stephenville TOWER XY-ABC frequency check
questions.explanation: 'Radio check 118.7' e la fraseologia standard per una trasmissione di prova volta a verificare la qualita delle comunicazioni.

questions.q: What is the Q-code for `magnetic bearing from the station`?
options: QTE
QDR
QDM
QFE
questions.explanation: QDR e il Q-code per il rilevamento magnetico dalla stazione.

questions.q: When may the name of the location or the call sign suffix in the call sign of an aeronautical station be omitted ?
options: Never
When satisfactory communication has been established and provided it will not be confusing to do so
Only after the aeronautical station has used the abbreviated call sign
In dense traffic during rush hours
questions.explanation: Il nome della localita o il suffisso del nominativo di una stazione aeronautica possono essere omessi solo dopo che e stata stabilita una comunicazione soddisfacente e non vi sia rischio di confusione.

questions.q: Which phrase shall be used if you want to say: `I should like to know...` or `I wish to obtain...`?
options: Report
Request
Acknowledge
Confirm
questions.explanation: 'Request' e la fraseologia standard per esprimere 'desidero sapere' o 'desidero ottenere'.

questions.q: What does the phrase `Read back` mean:
options: Let me know that you have received and understood this message
Did you correctly receive this message?
Repeat all, or the specified part, of this message back to me exactly as received
Check and confirm with originator
questions.explanation: 'Read back' significa ripetere integralmente, o nella parte specificata, il messaggio esattamente come ricevuto.

questions.q: Which is the frequency band containing frequencies of the Aeronautical Mobile Service?
questions.explanation: La banda di frequenze del servizio mobile aeronautico VHF e 118.000-136.975 MHz.

questions.q: What is the correct way of transmitting 1001 as a QNH ?
options: QNH one double `O` one
QNH one zero zero one
QNH one thousand and one
QNH one double zero one
questions.explanation: Il valore QNH 1001 si trasmette cifra per cifra: 'QNH one zero zero one'.

questions.q: What does the abbreviation `FIR` mean?
options: Flight information required.
Flow information received.
Flight information region.
Flight information radar.
questions.explanation: 'FIR' significa Flight Information Region (regione di informazioni di volo).

questions.q: Regardless of your position, time figures are transmitted with reference to:
options: Local time
Winter time
Greenwich time
UTC
questions.explanation: Gli orari si trasmettono sempre con riferimento al tempo coordinato universale (UTC), indipendentemente dalla posizione geografica.

questions.q: Which Q-code is used to report altitude ?
options: QFE
QFF
QNJ
QNH
questions.explanation: QNH e il Q-code utilizzato per riportare la quota (altitude) rispetto al livello del mare.

questions.q: If you are requested to report your height, to which Q-code-setting would you refer ?
options: QNH
QDM
QFE
QBI
questions.explanation: Per riportare l'altezza (height) sul terreno, si fa riferimento al QFE.

questions.q: In the event that a pilot is required to make a blind transmission, this should be made
options: Twice on the designated frequency
Only once on the designated frequency
On the emergency frequency only
During VFR flights only
questions.explanation: Una blind transmission va effettuata due volte sulla frequenza designata.

questions.q: On the readability scale what does `Readability 3` mean:
options: No problem to understand
Readable but with difficulty
Loud and clear
Unreadable
questions.explanation: Sulla scala di leggibilita, il valore 3 indica un segnale leggibile ma con difficolta.

questions.q: What is the Q-code for `true bearing from the station`?
options: QDR
QTE
QDM
QFE
questions.explanation: QTE e il Q-code per il rilevamento vero dalla stazione (true bearing).

questions.q: What is the radiotelephony call sign for the aeronautical station providing flight information service:
options: FLIGHT INFORMATION CENTRE
FLIGHT CENTRE
INFORMATION
CONTROL
questions.explanation: Il nominativo radiotelefonico per il servizio informazioni volo e 'INFORMATION'.

questions.q: Select the letter code for HB-FRO.
options: Hotel Bravo Fox Roma Olka
Hector Brasil Fox-trot Romeo Oscar
Hotel Bravo Fox-trot Romeo Oscar
Hotel Bravo Foxy Romeo Oscar
questions.explanation: Nell'alfabeto fonetico ICAO, HB-FRO si compita Hotel Bravo Foxtrot Romeo Oscar.

questions.q: What does the phrase `Go ahead` mean:
options: Taxi on
Proceed with your message
Pass me the following information...
Yes
questions.explanation: 'Go ahead' significa 'procedete con il vostro messaggio'.

questions.q: To which frequency bands do the frequencies 118.000 - 136.975 MHz of the Aeronautical Mobile Service belong?
options: Very low frequency
Low frequency
Very high frequency
Medium frequency
questions.explanation: La banda 118.000-136.975 MHz appartiene alle frequenze molto alte (Very High Frequency, VHF).

questions.q: Select the air traffic service in charge of control of local traffic, take-offs and landings at an airport
options: Radar
Control
Air Traffic Centre
Tower
questions.explanation: La 'TOWER' e l'ente responsabile del controllo del traffico locale, dei decolli e degli atterraggi su un aeroporto.

questions.q: When an aircraft station is unable to establish communication due to receiver failure, the following procedure should be undertaken:
options: Transmit at regular times or positions, the phrase `Transmitting blind due to receiver failure`.
Transmit reports at scheduled times or positions, preceded by the phrase `Transmitting blind due to receiverfailure`.
Change to frequency 121.5 MHZ and squawk 7700
Land at the nearest uncontrolled airfield.
questions.explanation: In caso di avaria al ricevente, si trasmettono riporti agli orari previsti, preceduti dalla frase 'transmitting blind due to receiver failure'.

questions.q: RADAR instructs aircraft XY-ABC: `X-BC reset squawk 1015`. What does this mean:
options: X-BC is requested to set new code 1015
X-BC is requested to squawk IDENT
X-BC has been identified at 10:15 (UTC)
X-BC is requested to reselect SSR code 1015
questions.explanation: 'Reset squawk 1015' istruisce il pilota a riselezionare sul transponder il codice SSR 1015.

questions.q: How shall a pilot inform the control tower that he is prepared for take-off:
options: Ready for take-off
Ready to line-up
Ready to go
Ready for departure or ready
questions.explanation: Per comunicare alla torre di essere pronti al decollo si usa 'ready for departure' (o semplicemente 'ready').

questions.q: You have been calling a station without getting an answer. How long time it is recommended to wait at least before making a second call?
options: 10 sec.
Wait until the station calls you
30 sec.
3 sec
questions.explanation: Prima di effettuare una seconda chiamata senza risposta, e raccomandabile attendere almeno 10 secondi.

questions.q: When the term `Overcast` is used in an aviation routine weather report (METAR), the amount of clouds covering the sky is
options: 50% or more
Less than 50%
No clouds but poor ground visibility
questions.explanation: 'Overcast' indica cielo completamente coperto (8 ottavi, 100%).

questions.q: Which phrase shall be used if you want to say: `An error has been made in this transmission (or message indicated). The correct version is ...`:
options: QNH 1017, negative QNH 1016
QNH 1017, negative 1016
QNH 1017, correction QNH 1016
QNH 1017, negative I say again 1016
questions.explanation: 'Correction' e la fraseologia standard per segnalare un errore in una trasmissione e fornire il valore corretto.

questions.q: The frequency used for the first transmission of a `MAYDAY` call shall be:
options: The distress frequency 121.5 MHz
The frequency currently in use
Any other international emergency frequency
Any frequency at pilot's discretion
questions.explanation: La prima trasmissione di una chiamata MAYDAY va effettuata sulla frequenza in uso al momento dell'emergenza, non necessariamente su una frequenza di emergenza dedicata.

questions.q: RADAR instructs aircraft X-BC: `X-BC squawk standby `. What does this mean?
options: X-BC is requested to standby on the frequency
X-BC is requested to standby for radar vectors
X-BC is requested to switch to standby position
X-BC is requested to standby as the radar controller is busy
questions.explanation: 'Squawk standby' istruisce il pilota a portare il transponder in posizione standby, sospendendo temporaneamente le risposte.

questions.q: The clearance: `cleared for immediate take-off runway 03` is:
options: An urgency message
An unauthorized message.
A flight safety message
A flight regularity message
questions.explanation: Un'autorizzazione come 'cleared for immediate take-off' e classificata come messaggio di sicurezza del volo.

questions.q: What is the radiotelephony call sign suffix for the aeronautical station indicating aerodrome control service:
options: CONTROL
AERODROME
TOWER
APRON
questions.explanation: 'TOWER' e il suffisso del nominativo per l'ente che fornisce il servizio di controllo di aerodromo.

questions.q: The message to an aeronautical ground station `please call a taxi-cab for us. We will arrive at 1045` is:
options: A flight regularity message
An unauthorized message.
A flight safety messages.
An urgency message.
questions.explanation: Un messaggio relativo a servizi personali (es. prenotare un taxi) non rientra tra le comunicazioni autorizzate del servizio mobile aeronautico.

questions.q: What does the instruction `Orbit right` mean ?
options: Make 360° turns to the right
Turn right to avoid other traffic
Right-hand circuits are in use
Leave the runway to the right
questions.explanation: 'Orbit right' istruisce l'aeromobile a effettuare virate complete di 360 gradi verso destra, tipicamente per attendere in un punto.

questions.q: What is the correct way of expressing visibility ?
options: Visibility 1.2 nautical miles
Visibility 1200 metres
Visibility 12 kilometres
Visibility 1200 feet
questions.explanation: La visibilita inferiore al chilometro si esprime in metri: 'visibility 1200 metres'.

questions.q: When the aircraft registration marking is used as a call-sign, your first contact with a station shall be in the following form:
options: The first letter and the last two characters of the aircraft registration marking.
The last three characters of the aircraft registration marking
The first three characters of the aircraft registration marking
All characters of the aircraft registration marking.
questions.explanation: Quando si usa la marca di immatricolazione come nominativo, il primo contatto con una stazione deve impiegare tutti i caratteri per intero.

questions.q: When shall the pilot of an aircraft experiencing communications failure keep a watch for instructions passed by visual signals ?
options: When flying VFR above clouds
When the aircraft is forming part of the aerodrome traffic at a controlled aerodrome
When the aircraft is entering the traffic pattern of an uncontrolled airport
When entering a FIR during an IFR flight
questions.explanation: Il pilota deve tenere d'occhio eventuali segnali visivi quando l'aeromobile fa parte del traffico di un aeroporto controllato (es. in caso di avaria radio).

questions.q: An aircraft in state of emergency shall squawk:
questions.explanation: Il codice 7700 sul transponder indica una condizione di emergenza a bordo.

questions.q: Which is the maximum distance at which you might expect solid VHF contact over flat terrain at flight level 100:
options: About 300 NM
About 120 NM
About 30 NM
About 12 NM
questions.explanation: Su terreno pianeggiante, a FL100 il contatto VHF solido e possibile fino a circa 120 NM.

questions.q: Which of the following abbreviated call signs of Cherokee XY-ABC is correct:
options: Cherokee BC
Cherokee XY-BC
Cherokee X-BC
Cherokee X-ABC
questions.explanation: Il nominativo abbreviato per un aeromobile di tipo Cherokee si forma con il nome del tipo seguito dalle ultime due lettere della marca (Cherokee BC).

questions.q: An urgency message shall be preceded by the radiotelephony urgencysignal:
options: URGENCY, spoken three times
MAYDAY, spoken three times
PAN PAN, spoken three times
ALERFA, spoken three times
questions.explanation: Un messaggio di urgenza e preceduto dal segnale radiotelefonico PAN PAN, ripetuto tre volte.

questions.q: What does the abbreviation `AFIS` mean?
options: Automatic flight information service.
Aerodrome flight information service.
Aeronautical flight information system.
Aerodrome flashing identification signal.
questions.explanation: 'AFIS' significa Aerodrome Flight Information Service (servizio informazioni volo di aerodromo).

questions.q: An aircraft is flying north-east at 2500 feet. TOWER requests heading and level. What is the correctresponse
options: Heading north-east at level 25
Heading 45 at 2500 feet
Heading 045 at 2500 feet
questions.explanation: La risposta corretta a una richiesta di prua e livello riporta entrambi i valori con la fraseologia standard: prua a tre cifre e quota in piedi ('heading 045 at 2500 feet').

questions.q: When the term `Broken` is used in an aviation routine weather report (METAR), the amount of clouds covering the sky is:
options: 5 to 7 octas
1 to 4 octas
8 octas below 10000 feet
No clouds below 5000 feet
questions.explanation: 'Broken' indica una copertura nuvolosa di 5-7 ottavi del cielo.

questions.q: Which word or phrase shall be used if you want to say: `Wait and I will call you`?
options: Go ahead
Standby
Roger
Wilco
questions.explanation: 'Standby' significa 'attendete, vi richiamero'.

questions.q: Which frequency shall be used for the first transmission of an urgency call:
options: The international emergency frequency
The regional guard frequency
The air-ground frequency in use at the time
Any frequency at pilot's discretion
questions.explanation: La prima trasmissione di una chiamata di urgenza va effettuata sulla frequenza aria-terra in uso al momento.

questions.q: The distress message shall contain as many as possible of the followingelements/details:
options: Aircraft call sign, route of flight, destination airport
Aircraft call sign, nature of distress, pilot's intention, present position, level and heading
Aircraft call sign, aerodrome of departure, position and level
Aircraft call sign, present position, assistance required
questions.explanation: Un messaggio di soccorso deve contenere, per quanto possibile: nominativo, natura dell'emergenza, intenzioni del pilota, posizione attuale, livello e prua.

questions.q: Shall an ATC route clearance always be read back:
options: No, if the ATC route clearance is transmitted in a published form (e.g. Standard Instrument Departure Route/SID)
No, if the communication channel is overloaded
Yes, unless authorized otherwise by ATS authority concerned
No, if the content of the ATC clearance is clear and no confusion is likely to arise
questions.explanation: Un'autorizzazione di rotta ATC va sempre ripetuta (read-back), salvo diversa autorizzazione dell'autorita ATS competente.

questions.q: Under which of the following circumstances shall an aircraft station squawk 7600 ?
options: When entering bad weather areas
When approaching a prohibited area
When flying over desert areas
In case of radio communication failure
questions.explanation: Un aeromobile trasmette il codice 7600 in caso di avaria alle comunicazioni radio.

questions.q: What does QDM mean?
options: Magnetic bearing from the station
True heading to the station (no wind)
Magnetic heading to the station (no wind)
True bearing from the station
questions.explanation: QDM e il Q-code per la prua magnetica verso la stazione (senza vento).

questions.q: What is the correct way to transmit and read back frequency 120.375 MHz (VHF channel separated by 25KHz):
options: One two zero decimal three seven
One twenty decimal three seven
One two zero decimal three seven five
One two zero three seven
questions.explanation: Su canali con spaziatura 25 kHz, 120.375 MHz si trasmette e ripete come 'one two zero decimal three seven', omettendo l'ultima cifra non significativa per la selezione del canale.

questions.q: What is the correct way of transmitting the number 3500 when indicating an altitude or an height ?
options: Three five zero zero
Three five hundred
Three five double zero
Three thousand five hundred
questions.explanation: Il numero 3500, riferito a un'altitudine o altezza, si trasmette come 'three thousand five hundred'.

questions.q: What is normally used for ATIS broadcasts ?
options: Voice channel of an ILS
Discrete VHF frequency or/and VOR
NDB frequencies
DME voice channel
questions.explanation: L'ATIS viene tipicamente diffuso sul canale vocale di un ILS, oltre che su frequenze VHF discrete o su un VOR.

questions.q: A message concerning an aircraft being threatened by grave and imminent danger, requiring immediate assistance is called:
options: Class B message
Flight safety message.
Urgency message.
Distress message.
questions.explanation: Un messaggio relativo a un aeromobile minacciato da un pericolo grave e imminente, che richiede assistenza immediata, e un messaggio di soccorso (distress message).
